title: Uchchi Veyilukku Song Lyrics Kummi Paatu
stanzas: <div>Uchchi Veyilukku Song Lyrics Kummi Paatu

Uchchi veyilukku
nachu podhaiyila
machaan marappu katturaa(2)

antha marappu kullaara
vachi morachi paakkuraan(2)

karisa kaattukulla
kudisa potu vachi (2)
pudhu pudhu aasaiyila
polusaaya kaathirukka

kanji kalayamthaan
kondaattam poduthu
un ingi iduppu mudippula
nalla valanji valanji
konji nadakkum nadaiyila

aattu kadaiyila
saaiyanthiram paakkayila
rathiri paakka varacholli
solliputtu poniye
ada solliputtu poniye

aaththaa urangale
appanum urangale
othayila unnaiya pakka
varamudiyalaye
aamaam varamudiyalaiye

Saama kozhi koovi mella
ooru sanam endhirichi
aalukkoru vaartha solli
Kaeli pechu pesirichi

adiyo .. vidum veetuku ponga
kalaiyedukka porappa naan
thoappukku vaaren
naa thoappukku vaaren

kanji kalayamthaan
kondaattam poduthu
un ingi iduppu mudippula
nalla valanji valanji
konji nadakkum nadaiyila

vachi morachi paakkuraan

polusaaya kaathirukka

Uchchi veyilukku
nachu podhaiyila
machaan marappu katturaa
antha marappu kullaara
vachi morachi paakkuraan

kaanja pullukulla
nee kaathu vachi pogaiyila
kaathula saththam kaetu
oadi vanthu paarthen
aamaa oadi vanthu paarthen

maentha maanukannu
veedu paathu pogaiyila
karutha maadu saththam
unna thaedi paarthen
aama unna thaedi paarthen

eera thalai enakku
...
nee varum vazhi paarthu
thinam thinam kaathiruppen

enna inimela yeikkathadi
ponnu kaetu vaaren
naan adhukkaaga appan
kitta pesi vaikka poren
nan pesi vaikka poren

kanji kalayamthaan
kondaattam poduthu
un ingi iduppu mudippula
nalla valanji valanji
konji nadakkum nadaiyila

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song Uchchi Veyilukku celebrates the joy and vibrancy of life, reflecting the cultural essence of Tamil folk traditions.

In which movie does this song appear?

This song is part of the Kummi Paattu genre, which is a traditional folk style, but the specific movie is unknown.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song features the talented voices of Arunmozhi and Swarnalatha, both renowned in the Tamil music industry.

When was the song released?

Uchchi Veyilukku was released on May 21, 1999.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Uchchi Veyilukku is a lively folk tune that embodies the spirit of celebration and joy. It captures the essence of Tamil culture through its engaging lyrics and upbeat rhythm. The song evokes feelings of nostalgia and happiness, making it perfect for festive occasions. With its catchy melody, it invites listeners to join in the fun and revelry of traditional Tamil festivities.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Uchchi Veyilukku is a vibrant folk number that captures the essence of Tamil culture with its lively beats and catchy lyrics. The collaboration of Arunmozhi and Swarnalatha brings an infectious energy that makes this song a timeless favorite. Dive into the joyous world of Kummi Paattu and let the music take you on a nostalgic journey!
stanzaMeanings: In the high sunlight, the girl dances, forgetting everything.
That forgetfulness is like a trap, watching closely.
In the dark, I put on a drink and wait for new desires.
The dance of the rice porridge is happening, your place is not finished, it will move well and smoothly.
In the dance shop, I see the night coming, I tell you to come, yes, I tell you to come.
Oh, everyone is sleeping, you are also sleeping, but I can't see you coming.
The rooster crows softly, the village is waking up, telling people to listen and speak.
Oh, go to your house, I will come to the festival, I will come to the festival.
The dance of the rice porridge is happening, your place is not finished, it will move well and smoothly.
In the high sunlight, the girl dances, forgetting everything. That forgetfulness is like a trap, watching closely.
In the dark, I put on a drink and wait for new desires.
In the high sunlight, the girl dances, forgetting everything. That forgetfulness is like a trap, watching closely.
In the grass, you are going with the wind, I heard a sound and came running, yes, I came running.
In the house of the man, I looked at the wind, searching for the sound of the cow, yes, searching for you.
Oh, my head is heavy... I will wait day by day for you to come.
From now on, don't call me, I will go to my father to talk about this.
The dance of the rice porridge is happening, your place is not finished, it will move well and smoothly.
In the high sunlight, the girl dances, forgetting everything. That forgetfulness is like a trap, watching closely.
In the dark, I put on a drink and wait for new desires.
